candidates.why: Explicit operating D&A line exists, but it is not labelled as cost of sales/COGS and appears as a separate operating expense line alongside personnel and other external costs rather than as a COGS-specific item. Relative to the selected COGS bucket ('Raw materials and supplies'), this does not clearly belong to COGS.
Component of the total D&A line, but not identified as COGS-related. Using it for COGS would be speculative and risks misclassification.
Component of the total D&A line, but not identified as COGS-related. More likely operating overhead/intangible amortisation than production cost.

variables.notes: Selected COGS reference bucket is only 'Raw materials and supplies'. No explicit COGS-specific depreciation/amortisation row is shown above financial lines. The visible D&A rows are separate operating lines but not attributable to COGS, so the conservative no-double-counting choice is to add nothing to COGS.

candidates.why: Best summary D&A row above operating profit. It sits as a separate same-level operating expense line outside the selected XSGA component rows ('Personnel costs', 'Other external costs', 'Other Operating Expenses'), so it should be added separately rather than assumed embedded. Preferred over component rows to avoid double counting.
Component of the summary D&A row. Do not choose when the total 'Depreciation and amortization' row is present.
Component of the summary D&A row. Do not choose when the total 'Depreciation and amortization' row is present.
Separate adjustment-style row with values only in some years; likely not the primary operating D&A line and would risk double counting alongside the main summary row.

variables.notes: The selected XSGA_COMPONENTS bucket excludes depreciation/amortization by prior mapping notes. Here, 'Depreciation and amortization' is presented as a distinct operating line above financial items, at the same level as the chosen overhead rows, so it appears outside the selected XSGA bucket and should be added separately. XRD is empty, so there is no separate R&D row creating a carve-out issue.
